methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: SendCutSend
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Isembard is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company founded by Alexander Fitzgerald that has raised $50M to date. The broader robotics and manufacturing sector includes one public comparable and eight private peers, including Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, Bedrock Robotics, and Dexterity. However, the peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or narrowly adjacent-stage comparables with aligned valuation tiers to support a reliable median-based valuation.

The Stage-Progression method is the applicable triangulation approach. Using total raised ($50M) as a baseline value proxy and applying a typical 2.0x step-up multiplier from Series A to the subsequent financing round yields an implied valuation of $100M. This method captures the capital-efficiency signal embedded in the company's fundraising history and typical venture outcomes at this stage.

The triangulated valuation range is $85M (low) to $115M (high), with a median of $100M. This range derives from a single quantitative method, reflecting the constraint that peer comparables are insufficient in both quantity and stage alignment to support multi-method triangulation. Key uncertainties include the applicability of the 2.0x multiplier to this company's specific market position, unit economics, and path to Series B readiness. Additional comparable data or forward revenue projections would strengthen conviction in this range.
